LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUDULENT CONCEALMENT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THERE ARE NO FACTS TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT CONCEALED INFORMATION WITH INTENT TO DEFRAUD PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONSTRUCTIVE FRAUD IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT SHOW THAT IT WAS DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEES WHO INSTRUCTED PLAINTIFF TO STOP MAKING LOAN PAYMENTS. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alison Betowsky, Attorney at Law, 714-852-6800, appearing by Coutcall for defendant JP Morgan Chase Bank. No other appearances.

The moving party appeared and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to first cause of action. Overruled as to second cause of action. Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay. Not reported. (D501)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Alyson Dudkowski of AlvaradoSmith APC (714.852.6800) appearing via Court Call for defendant and moving party; Tiffany R. Norman of TRN Law Associates (823.4566) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court modified its ruling as follows: demurrer to the 1st cause of action for Fraudulent Concealment is sustained with leave to amend. There are no facts to show that defendant concealed information with intent to defraud plaintiff. Defendant's demurrer to the 2nd cause of action for Constructive Fraud is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts that show that it was defendant's employees who instructed plaintiff to stop making loan payments. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
